subject: Re: CTF correction for EMDB deposition
body:
Hello Dr. Righetto,
This was helpful!
Thank you very much for the clarification,
Abhipsa

On Tue, Dec 19, 2023 at 4:15 AM Ricardo Righetto <ricardorighetto@gmail.com>
wrote:

> Dear Abhipsa,
>
> I agree this is very confusing. Many people misunderstand the difference
> between "estimating defocus" (fitting a CTF model to your experimental
> data) and "CTF correction", which is compensating for the effects of the
> CTF in some way (and there are different ways, as the EMDB options
> suggest). When EMDB asks this question about CTF correction, it is not
> about Warp, which you may have used for estimating the defocus of your
> micrographs. It wants to know what kind of correction was applied to the
> particle boxes when reconstructing your 3D map. If you used e.g. RELION or
> cryoSPARC for obtaining your 3D reconstruction, the answer is "phase
> flipping and amplitude correction". If you used other packages for
> processing your data, you have to check what type of correction is applied
> there.
>
> Best wishes,
> Ricardo
>
> --
> Ricardo Diogo Righetto
>
>
> Em qui., 7 de dez. de 2023 às 20:01, Abhipsa Shatarupa <sabhipsa@gmail.com>
> escreveu:
>
>> Hello,
>>
>> I am currently in the EMDB deposition process and have a question
>> regarding the "Correction type" field. The available options are "phase
>> flipping and amplitude correction" and "phase flipping only." Considering
>> that I checked the CTF box during particle picking, and the amplitude value
>> was set to 0.07, while leaving the phase shift box unchecked, could you
>> guide me on the correct choice between the provided options?
>>
>> Thank you, Abhipsa
>>
>> --
